result.markdown:
--- title: "001 — Descriptive baseline" date: 2026-06-11 --- ## Question What does India's PV market look like at state grain — size distribution, growth, fuel mix, seasonality, and OEM concentration? This baseline anchors every later experiment. ## Setup ::: {#d2398788 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import duckdb import plotly.express as px from complexity_lab.config import settings from complexity_lab.analysis import descriptive, distributions con = duckdb.connect(str(settings.db_path), read_only=True) year_panel = con.execute("SELECT * FROM panel_state_year").df() states = year_panel[year_panel.state_code != "ALL"] LATEST_FULL = int(states.year.max()) - 1 # last year is partial ``` ::: ## Market size and growth ::: {#39ce624b .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} all_india = year_panel[year_panel.state_code == "ALL"].set_index("year") px.bar(all_india.reset_index(), x="year", y="total_regs", title="All-India PV registrations by calendar year") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ::: {#223a467b .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} summary = descriptive.summary_table(states) summary.head(12).style.format({ "total_regs": "{:,.0f}", "cagr_5y": "{:.1%}", "ev_share": "{:.1%}", "cng_share": "{:.1%}", "hhi_oem": "{:,.0f}"}) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ```{=html}
  state_name latest_year total_regs cagr_5y ev_share cng_share hhi_oem
state_code              
MH Maharashtra 2025 562,926 16.3% 5.4% 36.7% 2,011
UP Uttar Pradesh 2025 505,974 14.5% 2.7% 29.7% 2,535
GJ Gujarat 2025 385,473 11.7% 3.0% 39.5% 2,211
HR Haryana 2025 321,906 17.3% 1.4% 33.7% 2,345
KA Karnataka 2025 321,292 13.0% 6.6% 12.7% 1,841
TN Tamil Nadu 2025 307,188 15.1% 4.9% 10.1% 1,970
RJ Rajasthan 2025 257,432 13.9% 4.4% 24.7% 2,425
KL Kerala 2025 246,382 9.4% 7.9% 4.9% 2,836
MP Madhya Pradesh 2025 207,472 13.1% 2.4% 23.9% 2,417
DL Delhi 2025 194,263 10.4% 7.6% 26.6% 1,871
PB Punjab 2025 179,496 15.6% 2.1% 6.8% 2,029
WB West Bengal 2025 118,437 9.0% 4.8% 8.6% 2,397
``` ::: ::: ## Size distribution: how unequal are states? ::: {#8a8fd547 .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} sizes = states[states.year == LATEST_FULL].set_index("state_code")["total_regs"] zipf = distributions.zipf_exponent(sizes) gini = distributions.gini(sizes) rs = distributions.rank_size(sizes) fig = px.scatter(rs, x="log_rank", y="log_size", title=f"Rank–size, {LATEST_FULL} — slope {zipf['slope']:.2f} (R²={zipf['r2']:.2f}), Gini {gini:.2f}") fig.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: A slope much steeper than −1 means registrations concentrate in top states faster than Zipf's law would imply — consistent with car markets tracking income and urbanization, not just population. ## Seasonality ::: {#561ce1d2 .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} month_panel = con.execute( "SELECT * FROM panel_state_month WHERE state_code = 'ALL' ORDER BY year, month").df() prof = descriptive.seasonality_profile(month_panel[month_panel.year.between(2013, LATEST_FULL)]) px.bar(prof.reset_index(), x="month", y="seasonal_index", error_y="std", title="All-India month-of-year index (1.0 = average month)") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## OEM concentration over time ::: {#4e34dd21 .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} edges = con.execute("SELECT * FROM oem_state_edges").df() conc = descriptive.concentration_series(edges, "year", "maker").reset_index() px.line(conc[conc.year <= LATEST_FULL], x="year", y="hhi", markers=True, title="National OEM HHI (10000 = monopoly)") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Finding The market roughly doubled from ~2.4M (2013) to ~4.4M (2025) with a visible COVID trough in 2020. State sizes are strongly unequal (Gini ≈ 0.6; rank–size slope ≈ −1.9, steeper than Zipf). Registrations peak around the festive months with a March fiscal-year-end bump. OEM concentration (HHI ≈ 2200) has been declining slowly as Maruti's share erodes toward SUV-heavy challengers — the structural question experiment 003 picks up at network grain.
